fields.description:
## What

Add a CI job that **drift-proofs the README quickstart's command TEXT** by extracting the
CLI fenced command block from `README.md` and executing those exact lines end-to-end
(init → create → ready → claim → close) in a throwaway git repo against a clean install.
This is the core maintenance-burden reducer for the P0 defects: a wrong command *printed*
in the README (e.g. `session-log` missing its `append` verb, or a hard-coded id) then
exits non-zero and FAILS the build.

**Why extract-and-execute (not `probe-rebar.sh` alone, and not a static `--help` check).**
`scripts/probe-rebar.sh` already exercises the golden path, but it uses its own
internally-correct syntax — it can NOT catch wrong text printed in the README. A static
"parse the block and check each command name against `rebar --help`" would catch a bad
command *name* but NOT a wrong *subcommand/argument* (the actual P0 defect: `session-log`
is a real command, but `session-log "<entry>"` is a wrong invocation) nor a stale
hard-coded id, nor ordering/state errors. Only running the literal README lines against
the live engine catches all of these. So this ticket adds a small **extractor** that pulls
the fenced block(s) from the README and runs them; `probe-rebar.sh` is retained as the
COMPLEMENTARY broader end-to-end smoke (kept wired, or invoked alongside) so it does not
become stranded/stale.

- Add `scripts/check_readme_quickstart.sh` (or a small Python extractor) that parses the
  ```bash fenced quickstart block from `README.md` and executes those exact lines in an
  isolated temp `REBAR_ROOT` / throwaway git repo. No network, no shared state.
- Wire it as a gating job in `.github/workflows/test.yml`, running against an INSTALLED
  rebar (clean `pipx`/`pip install`), so it also catches packaging/entry-point regressions.
- Keep `scripts/probe-rebar.sh` as the broader golden-path smoke (either invoked by the
  same job or left as-is) so its coverage is not lost.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A CI job extracts the README quickstart's CLI fenced block and runs those exact
      lines end-to-end; it FAILS the build if any of init/create/ready/claim/close changes
      exit code or the extracted commands error.
- [ ] The job runs against a CLEAN, non-editable install — the new `golden-path`
      job in `test.yml` AND `gerrit-verify.yaml` does `pip install .` (a built wheel,
      not editable) and runs `check_readme_quickstart.py` against it, so
      packaging/entry-point regressions (MANIFEST, package data, console-script
      registration) — which an editable install cannot catch — fail the build.
      Verify (prints nothing): `for f in .github/workflows/test.yml .github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml; do grep -qF 'golden-path' "$f" && grep -qF 'pip install .' "$f" || echo "NO CLEAN-INSTALL JOB: $f"; done`
- [ ] The README *text* itself is what executes: a wrong command *printed* in the README
      — e.g. `session-log` missing its `append` verb, or a disconnected hard-coded id —
      is demonstrably caught (note the manual verification in a comment). Executability of
      an independent probe is NOT sufficient (closes epic finding G6/F4).
- [ ] The CI golden-path is the trimmed init->create->ready->claim->close subset the
      ticket explicitly permits — `scripts/check_readme_quickstart.py` (which runs
      `rebar init` explicitly and passes non-interactively) — NOT the full
      `probe-rebar.sh`. The broader probe was found to have PRE-EXISTING rot (relies
      on TTY-only auto-init + 10 stale assertions) that is out of this docs epic's
      scope; its fix-and-wire is tracked as discovered follow-up bug
      `slushy-commonlaw-drongo` (linked `discovered_from` this ticket). So T7 is
      addressed by tracking the rot honestly rather than wiring a red probe.

Depends on `a41f` (session-log fix) and `b55f` (quickstart repaired to a runnable
single-ticket block) — this gate extracts and runs the README quickstart, so it must land
AFTER those fix the text, or it would extract currently-broken commands and fail for the
wrong reason. (Dependency edges declared.)

Files: `scripts/check_readme_quickstart.sh` (new extractor), `scripts/probe-rebar.sh`
(retained/complementary), `.github/workflows/test.yml`.
